title: Arumeru District Council (Water use Tariffs) By-laws, 2003
title_en: Arumeru District Council (Water use Tariffs) By-laws, 2003

provisions.title: Title and application
provisions.content: These By-laws may be cited as the Arumeru District Council (Water use Tariffs) By-laws, 2003 and shall apply in specified Trading Centers and Major water projects.

provisions.title: Interpretation
provisions.content: For purpose of these By-laws:— “ Act ” means Local Government (District Authorities) Act, 1982; “ Beneficiaries ” means the members of the village, ward or division who use water from a specified project; “ Council ” means Arumeru District Council “ Donors ” means and includes any person, Company, association, or any corporate body which financed the project; “ Major projects ” means and includes, mount Meru gravity water supply project, Mbuguni Pipeline, Mateves pipeline (Old TPDF pipeline); “ Specified Trading Centers ” means and includes Usa River, Tengeru and Sekei

provisions.title: Management of project
provisions.content: (1) All major project financed or managed by donors. Shall firstly be handled to the council and beneficiaries. (2) Without prejudice to by-laws 3(1) above, after the expiration of two years period from the time which project was managed by council and beneficiaries, the project shall be handled over to and managed by beneficiaries.

provisions.title: Payment of tariff
provisions.content: Tariff prescribed by the Council in accordance with the schedule hereto shall be paid by every person who connected water to his premises.

provisions.title: Disconnection of water
provisions.content: (1) Any person who fails to pay the tariff prescribed under those by-laws within three consecutive months, wither such tariff is legally demanded or not, council shall disconnect water from his premises. (2) A council shall, on application made to it by a person on whose premises water was so disconnected, shall require such person to pay reconnection fee not less than 10,000/=. (3) Any person whose debt exceed 200,000 Tanzanian shillings, shall pay reconnection fee of 5% of the total debt.

(1) No person shall be allowed to reconnect water to his premises, save for applying and obtaining written consent from council. (2) All reconnection process shall be done under supervision of Council ’s staffs (3) Any person who reconnect water to his premises contrary to bylaws 3(1) and (2) above commits an offence and is liable on conviction to a fine of fifty thousand shillings or imprisonment for twelve months or to both fine and imprisonment.

provisions.title: Offences
provisions.content: Any person who willful destroy water meter or causes damage to such meter which renders it incapable of shall be liable on conviction to fine not exceeding three hundred thousand shillings or imprisonment for twelve months or to both fine and imprisonment. Schedule Water tariff for year 2003 Domestic premises (2) Gravity 2,000/= (3) Pumping 3,000/= Business premises (2) Gravity 4,000/= (3) Pumping 4,000/= History of this document 25 April 2003 this version Published in Gazette of the United Republic of Tanzania 17 Commenced 17 March 2003 Assented to Related documents More documents like this one [] [] [] [] ▲ To the top > Contact us Visit our Twitter/X profile Visit our Linked In profile Visit our YouTube channel About TanzLII TanzLII is operated by the Judiciary of Tanzania and the Office of the Attorney General, Chief Parliamentary Draftsman, and publishes the law of Tanzania for free online access for all.
